Extrae los elementos principales del siguiente texto: "hello and welcome to the video presentation of our paper on analyzinglanguage models for metaphor recognition. We tried to find out whycurrent systems perform well on this task. So at first, a little exercise.To understand what this task is about, please find all the metaphors in thefollowing two sentences.Looking at the results, you might have noticed that in the second sentence, itis far more easy to identify the correct words as metaphors than in thefirst sentence. But before diving deeper into the analysis, we first needa definition of what metaphors actually are.So, metaphors perform a meaning transfer, so, they transfer semantic propertiesfrom one conceptual domain to another. So, here in the first example we see 'heattacked my argument'. Here the word 'detect', which originates in the domainof WAR or FIGHT is applied in the domain of ARGUMENT. The same patternalso, uh, exists for example two and three, whereas example two might not beused as often as example one. So, 'bashed' might be not as conventional in usageas 'attacked'. So there is a spectrum of conventionality.Based on this definition, we try to define some requirements for metaphorrecognition systems, so they should recognize both, conventionalized andnovel metaphors. Furthermore, also recognize metaphor patterns onrarer word types and, of course, should also generalize across synonyms andmorphological variations within these patterns. Or as Ekaterina Shutovaput it in 2015: Metaphor recognition systems should not solve Word SenseDisambiguationWe find that current systems heavily rely on language models and wordembeddings. So we have to ask if they really fulfill the requirements formetaphor recognition systems.Now for the models that we considered in our analysis. At first, we have thebaseline, which is based on training statistics. So it assigns the 'metaphor'label if it has seen the word under consideration more often labeledas metaphor in the training set. And otherwise it will assign 'literal' label.The system of Wu et al. is a Convolutional Neural Network, combined with an LSTMUsing word2vec word embeddings. This was the Shared Task winner onthe Metaphor Detection Shared Task 2018.Then we have three models that are based on the same base model, so theyall use bi-directional LSTMs with GloVe and ELMo beddings. Where Maoet al. use some additional lingual linguistic modeling and Stoweet al. use additional training data.Our approach is a fine-tuned BERT, Uh, Dankers et al. also use a fine-tuneBERT, but in a multitask learning set up where they would also learn emotionlabels, additionally, to the metaphor labels. And then we have three fine-tuned RoBERTa systems, beginning with ILLINIMET, which is a plain fine-tunedRoBERTa, then ILLINIMET-F, which uses a wide variety of linguistic features.And then DM of Su et al., which is the Shared Task winner of 2020. They alsouse a fine-tuned RoBERTa with part of speech features and some explicitcontext modeling.So, the considered tasks in our analysis are two data sets: the VUA-SEQ andthe VUA-ALL-POS. They are both based on the same initial corpus, whichis the VUA metaphor corpus from the University of Amsterdam, released in2010, uh, the VUA-SEQ evaluates on all tokens. So it's basically a sequencelabeling task where

I will argue that humanity's best days are not ahead of it. The unchecked growth of Neoliberal capitalism means that the future will be marked by more exploitation, not less. The factory may have disappeared, but people are expected to be available to work at all hours of the day instead. Studies show that people working from home work far more than people who work in office, because they feel pressured to as a result of working from home. Neoliberalism develops new technologies for social control that appear on its face lighter, and more gentle but are actually more pernicious and more compulsive.  Because of Neoliberalism's essential engine, growth, it appears totally unclear it will be capable of negotiating climate change. Instead, the answer will be the extension of brutal regimes of subordination, in the third world as well as in the imperial core, to encourage the apparatus of production far past the point that the earth refuses to render use value to the flow of capital. Only a radical upheaval of the system of valorization, that is the abolition of the commodity form, would be adequate in averting this catastrophe, barring some sort of technological miracle (roughly akin to Hitler's wunderwaffe)--even in this last case Neoliberalism would merely generate some new, inevitably crisis.  In conclusion, humanity's best days are not ahead of it. The project has failed.  Context: Please provide a comprehensive and detailed response to the following. Consider all relevant aspects, background context, edge cases, and practical implications. Include concrete examples where appropriate.
There is no argument for a "nice capitalism." The fetish of the commodity does not operate the way it does because it's mean, or because mean people perpetuate it. Unchecked, accelerating growth is the only way for capitalism to sustain itself. Ambitions for a "more equitable organization of capitalism" engage in a mute actionism because it misapprehends the site of politics, which occurs in the production of subjectivity. The only way to argue that humanity's best days are ahead of it is to argue for the abolition of the mode of valorization.  As to technology, it is possible that we are saved by some Ex Machina, some incredible way to produce power or what have you, but this implies another important topic. When Richard Gatling invented the Gatling gun, he did so in the hope that it would reduce the casualties in war, because less people would need to be in an army to exercise the devastation of a large one. The lesson is this: even if some miraculous mode of energy production is found, capitalism can more than readily exploit this for an even more brutal, more pernicious form of exploitation. Technological innovations are always open to capture by the mode of valorization, and indeed can extend it in terrifying ways. One may very well argue that technological innovations will further territorialize the field of life that it generates. Very well. To add as a last point, we can agree that there may be some better future come the abolition of the commodity form. What I argue finally is that "humanity" will not survive the arrival of Communism. The "human" is already an administrative category, that which delimits the human and the inhuman. The human sciences extend the apparatus of knowledge-power, and indeed therefore disability, and also animal exploitation.  The solution is therefore clear. The abolition of the commodity form at once coincides with the abolition of the human. Something new will arrive, a new people, something better in the next life. Therefore, as the final conclusion to my argument, "humanity's" best days are not ahead of it.  Context: Please provide a comprehensive and detailed response to the following. Here is the final part. Part 3 --- Here we will be showing how the principles and practices we’ve covered so far converge into one over-arching result that will benefit you for the rest of your life. You can think of it as flipping a switch that changes how you create new results in life one day at a time. This is at the very core of what we call Day Sculpting. The simplest way to think of it is that most of the way we live is habitual. You have an habitual way of brushing your teeth, walking, talking to yourself and others, eating, working. Habits are wonderful…they make life easy but they also limit you. For example, if you have a habit of eating too much, you’ll put on weight. Not instantly, but steadily, day by day, until one day you have a weight problem. If you try to change your weight quickly through a trendy new diet, research shows that the weight is likely to come back, and then some, within a few short months, because the habits required to live at your ideal weight have not been properly established. Habits are habits because you don’t think about them, they happen nonconsciously. If you want a change in your life, you have to embody the change at a nonconscious level, so that the habits keeping your life the way it is today begin to shift. Wouldn’t it be great if there was a switch in the brain that would move you from status quo to status GO!? This is a switch that once you flip it will produce the result you want, if you are willing to commit to and stay with the process.Day Sculpting is your guide to fully realizing the success you are ready to enjoy. A critically important capacity of the human mind called preconscious processing. This is the ability of the mind to receive information, beneath our conscious awareness, and act upon it without even knowing that it is happening. Used correctly, this is an amazing power. Used improperly, it will sabotage your best efforts and make life extremely difficult. Most of us think we are running the show with our conscious awareness, consciously choosing our thoughts, behaviors, and emotions and consequently, we believe are able to choose the results we create in life. However, what neuro-science research shows, is that we all have a vast nonconscious mind that is really running the show most of the time. That deeper part of us, in charge of our habitual thinking, feeling, and behaving is always operating in our best interest. But it does so using information that may be faulty or outdated. If you continue to feed it information that doesn’t serve you, it will continue to habitually bring results that are less than desired. Your preconscious processor is constantly routing new information directly into this larger database that your mind uses to create new behaviors. Your job is to place the right information into this database every single day, so that it can draw upon

analyze this:   The Coming of Age story archetype involves a young protagonist who must navigate the challenges of growing up and discovering their place in the world. The Before-After-Bridge copywriting framework is designed to highlight the transformation that a person can experience after using a product or service.  The reason why these two frameworks work well together is that they both focus on transformation and growth. By combining them, you can create a powerful narrative that speaks to your audience's desire for personal development and improvement.  For example, imagine you are selling a personal development course that helps people overcome self-doubt and build self-confidence. By using the Coming of Age archetype, you can frame the course as a journey of self-discovery, where the customer will face challenges and obstacles, but ultimately emerge as a more confident and self-assured person.  Then, by using the Before-After-Bridge framework, you can show the customer what their life will be like after completing the course. You can highlight the benefits of increased self-confidence, such as improved relationships, better career opportunities, and greater overall happiness. By painting this picture of what's possible, you can create a sense of excitement and motivation that encourages the customer to take action and enroll in the course.  Overall, the Coming of Age story archetype and the Before-After-Bridge copywriting framework work well together because they tap into a fundamental human desire for growth and transformation. By combining these frameworks in your marketing messages, you can create a compelling narrative that speaks to your audience's deepest aspirations and motivates them to take action.
